subject: Human Resources
title: ERGs as DEI Infrastructure
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: ERGs as DEI Infrastructure.

hook.text: Xerox created the first ERG in 1970—the National Black Employees Caucus. It became a model copied by thousands of companies.

buildup.text: Employee Resource Groups serve as community builders, talent developers, and advisory bodies for DEI strategy.

discovery.text: Well-supported ERGs influence product design, hiring practices, and company policy—they're strategic assets, not social clubs.

twist.text: ERG leaders often do this work unpaid, on top of regular jobs—creating burnout among the very people they aim to support.

climax.text: Companies that compensate ERG leaders, fund their budgets, and connect them to executives see the greatest strategic impact.

punchline.text: ERGs built on volunteer labor exhaust their champions. Investment sustains them.

quiz.question: What risk do ERGs face without proper support?
options: Leaders burn out from doing unpaid work on top of their regular jobs
